meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn a proven client-led workflow to identify profitable SaaS ideas using Antigravity and Claude Code. Start building today.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for 'ideas' and start hunting for 'friction.' By auditing specific client workflows—identifying manual, repetitive tasks—you uncover high-value SaaS opportunities. Use Antigravity to map these processes and Claude Code to rapidly prototype solutions. This turns billable service hours into scalable, recurring product revenue.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. Most founders lose months agonizing over 'the next big idea,' while the real gold is buried in the mundane, manual workflows of the businesses around them. You don't need a stroke of genius; you need a sharp eye for inefficiency.

This case study shifts your focus from abstract ideation to concrete validation. By analyzing client workflows, you aren't just building software—you are building a solution to a problem that already has a budget. Let's transform those tedious client bottlenecks into your next high-margin SaaS MVP.

steps.title: Identify the Bottleneck
steps.description: Select a client or industry niche. Interview them to find the one task they hate doing every week. We look for 'spreadsheet hell' or data entry cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following client task: [Insert Task]. Break it down into discrete, repeatable steps and identify the exact point where human error or time-cost is highest.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Interview script for identifying manual workflow bottlenecks in [Niche]'

steps.title: Map the Data Flow
steps.description: Visualize how data moves from input to output. This reveals the logic required for your backend architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a visual flow map for a system that automates [Task]. Define the input triggers, processing logic, and the final output format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'create a mermaid.js flowchart representing the data path for [Task]'

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Strip away the fluff. Identify the core feature that solves the primary pain point. Avoid scope creep by focusing on the 'Golden Path'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define the minimum feature set to solve [Task] for a client. What is the one thing this tool must do to be worth a $99/mo subscription?
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'write a concise PRD for a MVP focusing only on [Core Feature]'

steps.title: Architect the Solution
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold your project structure. We prioritize speed-to-market using lightweight, scalable frameworks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project structure for a Next.js/Supabase application that automates [Task]. Include schema definitions for core tables.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold a new project in ./[ProjectName] with the architecture defined in the schema'

steps.title: Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Build the core loop first. Validate with the client immediately before adding UI polish or secondary features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic function for [Task] handling edge cases like empty inputs or data format errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'implement the primary service function in [File] and write unit tests for edge cases'

pro_tips: Always build for the 'lazy' user; if your software doesn't save them at least 5 hours a week, they won't pay.
Use 'Shadow Mode': Build the automation to run in the background for a client before presenting it as a full UI product.
Prioritize API-first development so your tool can integrate into their existing stack, reducing friction for adoption.
Collect testimonials during the MVP phase by offering the tool for free in exchange for a documented case study.
Don't build a dashboard until you've automated the data processing; nobody cares about charts if the work isn't done.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, implement a feedback loop with your pilot client, refine the workflow automation, and begin outreach to three more companies within the same niche to scale your recurring revenue.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop guessing and start building products that solve real, paid problems. It is time to stop being a coder and start being a founder who builds value. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
